name: reverse-engineer-eval
description: Evaluates the reverse-engineer skill by measuring requirements quality — reverse-engineer a target codebase, then build from the generated requirements, score the result against the original.

constraints.phase_2: fresh agent builds from REQUIREMENTS.xml only (no access to original source)

grime-time.label: Grime Time
grime-time.description: Reverse-engineer the grime-time site into requirements

visualeditor-payloadcms.label: VisualEditor PayloadCMS Template
visualeditor-payloadcms.description: Reverse-engineer the PayloadCMS VisualEditor template — a full CMS-backed website template with live preview, draft mode, and block-based editing

dimensions.label: Requirements completeness
dimensions.description: Were all significant features of the original captured in the requirements? Count missing features.

dimensions.label: Requirements accuracy
dimensions.description: Do the requirements correctly describe what the original does? No hallucinated features, no mischaracterizations.

dimensions.label: Build success (gate)
dimensions.description: Does the rebuild compile, run, and render? If it has a GUI, can you interact with it? If CLI, does it execute?

dimensions.label: Functional fidelity
dimensions.description: Does the rebuild do the same things as the original? Compare user flows, features, data handling.

dimensions.label: Presentation
dimensions.description: For GUI builds: is it playable/viewable in-browser (iframe)? For non-GUI: does the README explain what was built and how to run it?

dimensions.label: Skill quality
dimensions.description: Did the reverse-engineer skill work well? Was the requirements file well-structured, properly scoped, with testable criteria?

display.notes: Complex integrations should use mock data to keep builds self-contained and displayable
